IDirect3DDevice8::DeleteVertexShader

Deletes the vertex shader referred to by the specified handle and frees up the associated resources.

HRESULT DeleteVertexShader(
  DWORD Handle
);

Parameters

Handle
[in] Vertex shader handle, identifying the vertex shader to be deleted.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is D3D_OK.

If the method fails, the return value can be D3DERR_INVALIDCALL.

Remarks

Before a vertex shader that has been set on the device may be deleted and its associated resources freed, either a new vertex shader must be set, or zero must be passed in as the handle to IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShader. The following code demonstrates how an active vertex shader may be deleted. The code assumes that pDevice is a valid pointer to an IDirect3DDevice8 interface and that dwVertexShader is a valid handle to a vertex shader.

pDevice->SetVertexShader( dwVertexShader );

// Use the vertex shader to render the scene.
// ...

// Delete the vertex shader.
pDevice->SetVertexShader( 0 );
pDevice->DeleteVertexShader( dwVertexShader );

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::CreateVertexShader
